sutras.hindi_meaning: सत्त्व और पुरुष अत्यन्त असंकीर्ण (भिन्न) हैं; उनके प्रत्यय में अविशेष (अभेद) भोग है; परार्थत्व (पराधीनता) से स्वार्थ पर संयम करने से पुरुष-ज्ञान होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Distinguish between pure consciousness and the mind that reflects it. This discrimination is the key to knowing your true Self.

sutras.english_translation: From that arise intuitive hearing, touch, sight, taste, and smell.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उससे प्रातिभ (सहज), श्रावण (दिव्य श्रवण), वेदना (दिव्य स्पर्श), आदर्श (दिव्य दृष्टि), आस्वाद (दिव्य रस) और वार्ता (दिव्य गन्ध) उत्पन्न होते हैं।
sutras.practical_application: As discrimination deepens, all senses become refined and transcendent. Do not seek these powers; let them arise naturally from practice.

sutras.english_translation: These are obstacles to samadhi, though they are powers in the worldly state.
sutras.hindi_meaning: ये (सिद्धियाँ) समाधि में उपसर्ग (विघ्न) हैं, किन्तु व्युत्थान (सांसारिक) अवस्था में सिद्धियाँ हैं।
sutras.practical_application: Do not become attached to psychic powers. They are distractions from the ultimate goal. Stay focused on liberation, not abilities.

sutras.english_translation: By loosening the cause of bondage and by knowledge of the pathways, the mind can enter another body.
sutras.hindi_meaning: बन्ध-कारण (बाँधने के कारण) की शिथिलता और प्रचार (मार्ग) के संवेदन से चित्त का पर-शरीर में प्रवेश होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Understanding what binds consciousness to the body opens possibilities for expanded awareness. In practice, focus on loosening attachments.

sutras.english_translation: By mastering udana, one rises above water, mud, thorns, and such, and at death can ascend.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उदान-वायु पर विजय प्राप्त करने से जल, पंक (कीचड़), कण्टक (काँटों) आदि में असंग (लिप्त न होना) और उत्क्रान्ति (ऊर्ध्वगमन) होती है।
sutras.practical_application: Develop the upward-moving energy through practice. In daily life, this translates to buoyancy, lightness, and the ability to rise above difficulties.

sutras.english_translation: By mastering samana, effulgence is attained.
sutras.hindi_meaning: समान-वायु पर विजय से ज्वलन (दीप्ति/तेज) प्राप्त होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Balance the digestive fire and equalizing energy through pranayama. Good digestion and radiant health follow from samana mastery.

sutras.english_translation: By samyama on the relationship between the ear and space, divine hearing arises.
sutras.hindi_meaning: श्रोत्र (कान) और आकाश के सम्बन्ध पर संयम करने से दिव्य श्रोत्र (दिव्य श्रवण-शक्ति) प्राप्त होती है।
sutras.practical_application: Listen deeply to the silence within and around you. As inner listening deepens, subtler dimensions of sound become accessible.

sutras.english_translation: By samyama on the relationship between the body and space, and by absorption in the lightness of cotton, one can traverse space.
sutras.hindi_meaning: काय (शरीर) और आकाश के सम्बन्ध पर संयम तथा लघु तूल (रूई) की समापत्ति से आकाश-गमन (आकाश में चलना) सिद्ध होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Contemplate the spacious nature of the body and its connection to vast space. In practice, this cultivates lightness and freedom from physical limitation.

sutras.transliteration: bahir akalpitā vṛttir mahā-videhā tataḥ prakāśāvaraṇa-kṣayaḥ
sutras.english_translation: An unimagined external modification is the great bodiless state, from which the covering over light is destroyed.
sutras.hindi_meaning: बाह्य अकल्पित (वास्तविक) वृत्ति महाविदेहा है; उससे प्रकाश के आवरण का क्षय होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Transcend identification with the body through genuine out-of-body awareness. This removes the veil of ignorance over inner light.

sutras.english_translation: By samyama on the gross form, essential nature, subtle form, interconnection, and purposefulness of the elements, mastery over them is gained.
sutras.hindi_meaning: स्थूल, स्वरूप, सूक्ष्म, अन्वय (सम्बन्ध) और अर्थवत्त्व (प्रयोजन) पर संयम करने से भूत-जय (तत्त्वों पर विजय) होती है।
sutras.practical_application: Study the elements at every level — from gross physical to their subtle essence and purpose. Deep understanding leads to mastery.

sutras.english_translation: From that come the manifestation of powers like becoming minute, bodily perfection, and non-obstruction by the properties of elements.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उससे अणिमा आदि (सिद्धियों) का प्रादुर्भाव, काय-सम्पत् (शारीरिक पूर्णता) और तत्-धर्मों से अनभिघात (अबाधित रहना) होता है।
sutras.practical_application: These eight classical powers arise from elemental mastery. While extraordinary, remember they are stepping stones, not the destination.

sutras.english_translation: Bodily perfection consists of beauty of form, grace, strength, and adamantine hardness.
sutras.hindi_meaning: रूप, लावण्य (सौन्दर्य), बल और वज्र-संहननत्व (दृढ़ता) — ये काय-सम्पत् (शारीरिक सम्पदा) हैं।
sutras.practical_application: A perfected body radiates beauty, grace, strength, and resilience. These qualities naturally develop through dedicated yogic practice.

sutras.english_translation: By samyama on the power of perception, essential nature, I-sense, interconnection, and purpose, mastery over the senses is gained.
sutras.hindi_meaning: ग्रहण, स्वरूप, अस्मिता, अन्वय और अर्थवत्त्व पर संयम करने से इन्द्रिय-जय (इन्द्रियों पर विजय) होती है।
sutras.practical_application: Examine the process of perception at every level — the act, the nature, the ego, the connection, and the purpose — to gain complete sensory mastery.

sutras.english_translation: From that comes speed like the mind, functioning without instruments, and mastery over Pradhana (primordial nature).
sutras.hindi_meaning: उससे मनोजवित्व (मन की गति), विकरणभाव (इन्द्रियों के बिना कार्य) और प्रधान-जय (प्रकृति पर विजय) होती है।
sutras.practical_application: As sensory mastery deepens, consciousness operates beyond physical limitations. These represent the pinnacle of psychic development.

sutras.english_translation: Only from the knowledge of the distinction between Sattva and Purusha come supremacy over all states of being and omniscience.
sutras.hindi_meaning: सत्त्व और पुरुष के अन्यताख्यातिमात्र (केवल विवेक-ज्ञान) से सर्वभावाधिष्ठातृत्व (सबके ऊपर आधिपत्य) और सर्वज्ञातृत्व (सर्वज्ञता) होती है।
sutras.practical_application: The ultimate discrimination between consciousness and nature grants mastery over all existence and all knowledge. This is viveka at its peak.

sutras.english_translation: By non-attachment even to that, with the destruction of the seeds of all defects, comes kaivalya (liberation).
sutras.hindi_meaning: उसमें (सर्वज्ञता में) भी वैराग्य होने से दोष-बीज का क्षय होने पर कैवल्य (मोक्ष) होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Even the highest attainments must be released. True liberation comes only when you let go of everything, including spiritual powers and knowledge.

sutras.english_translation: When invited by celestial beings, one should avoid attachment and pride, for it leads to undesirable consequences.
sutras.hindi_meaning: स्थानी (देवों) के उपनिमन्त्रण (आमन्त्रण) पर सङ्ग (आसक्ति) और स्मय (अभिमान) नहीं करना चाहिए, क्योंकि पुनः अनिष्ट प्रसंग होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Even when you receive extraordinary recognition or temptation from higher realms, maintain humility. Attachment to status or praise leads to downfall.

sutras.english_translation: By samyama on the moment and its succession, discriminative knowledge is born.
sutras.hindi_meaning: क्षण और उसके क्रम पर संयम करने से विवेकजन्य ज्ञान उत्पन्न होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Meditate deeply on the nature of each moment and the flow of time. This reveals the truth of impermanence and leads to liberating discrimination.

sutras.english_translation: From that comes the ability to distinguish between two identical things when they cannot be differentiated by class, characteristic, or position.
sutras.hindi_meaning: जाति, लक्षण और देश से अन्यता (भेद) का अवच्छेद (निर्णय) न होने पर भी दो तुल्य (समान) वस्तुओं में उससे प्रतिपत्ति (भेद-ज्ञान) होती है।
sutras.practical_application: Viveka-born knowledge enables you to perceive unique distinctions even between seemingly identical things. This is the height of perceptual clarity.

sutras.sanskrit: तारकं सर्वविषयं सर्वथाविषयमक्रमं चेति विवेकजं ज्ञानम्
sutras.transliteration: tārakaṁ sarva-viṣayaṁ sarvathā-viṣayam akramaṁ ceti viveka-jaṁ jñānam
sutras.english_translation: The knowledge born of discrimination is the liberator; it encompasses all objects, all conditions, and is beyond sequence.
sutras.hindi_meaning: तारक (उद्धार करने वाला), सर्वविषय, सर्वथाविषय और अक्रम — ऐसा विवेकजन्य ज्ञान है।
sutras.practical_application: This all-encompassing, instantaneous wisdom liberates from all bondage. It is the fruit of the entire yogic path and transcends sequential understanding.

sutras.english_translation: When the purity of Sattva equals that of the Purusha, there is kaivalya (liberation).
sutras.hindi_meaning: सत्त्व और पुरुष के शुद्धि की समानता होने पर कैवल्य (मोक्ष) होता है।
sutras.practical_application: When the mind becomes as pure as consciousness itself, liberation is achieved. Purify the mind through practice until no separation remains.

padas.name: Kaivalya Pada
padas.theme: Liberation - The nature of absolute freedom

sutras.english_translation: Siddhis (powers) arise from birth, herbs, mantras, austerities, or samadhi.
sutras.hindi_meaning: जन्म, औषधि, मन्त्र, तप और समाधि से सिद्धियाँ उत्पन्न होती हैं।
sutras.practical_application: Powers can come from various sources, but only those from samadhi are spiritually transformative. Prioritize meditation over shortcuts.

sutras.english_translation: Transformation into another species occurs by the overflow of natural tendencies.
sutras.hindi_meaning: एक जाति से दूसरी जाति में परिणाम (रूपान्तर) प्रकृति के आपूर (प्रवाह) से होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Fundamental transformation happens through the natural flow of prakriti. Work with nature's tendencies rather than against them for lasting change.

sutras.transliteration: nimittam aprayojakaṁ prakṛtīnāṁ varaṇa-bhedas tu tataḥ kṣetrikavat
sutras.english_translation: The instrumental cause does not directly move the natural forces; it merely removes obstacles, like a farmer opening a sluice gate.
sutras.hindi_meaning: निमित्त (कारण) प्रकृतियों का प्रयोजक (चालक) नहीं है; वरण (बाधा) का भेद (हटाना) ही करता है, जैसे किसान (पानी की रुकावट दूर करता है)।
sutras.practical_application: You cannot force growth; you can only remove obstacles. Like a farmer who opens channels for water, clear the blocks and let nature do the rest.

sutras.english_translation: Created minds arise from the sense of I-am-ness alone.
sutras.hindi_meaning: निर्माण (निर्मित) चित्त केवल अस्मिता से उत्पन्न होते हैं।
sutras.practical_application: The ego creates multiple mental frameworks. Recognize that all constructed identities arise from the basic sense of 'I am.'

sutras.english_translation: Among the various created minds, one mind directs the many.
sutras.hindi_meaning: अनेक (निर्मित चित्तों) की प्रवृत्ति के भेद में प्रयोजक (चालक) चित्त एक है।
sutras.practical_application: Among your many mental states and sub-personalities, there is one core consciousness directing all. Identify with that central awareness.

sutras.english_translation: Of these, that which is born of meditation is free from karmic residue.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उनमें ध्यान से उत्पन्न (चित्त) आशय-रहित (कर्मसंस्कार से मुक्त) होता है।
sutras.practical_application: The mind refined by meditation does not create new karmic seeds. This is why meditation is the highest sadhana for liberation.

sutras.english_translation: The karma of a yogi is neither white nor black; for others it is threefold.
sutras.hindi_meaning: योगी का कर्म अशुक्ल-अकृष्ण (न सफ़ेद न काला) है; अन्यों के लिए तीन प्रकार का होता है।
sutras.practical_application: As you advance in yoga, your actions become free from the dualities of good and bad karma. Act from pure awareness rather than desire or aversion.

sutras.english_translation: From the three types of karma, only those latent impressions manifest whose fruition is appropriate.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उनसे उस विपाक के अनुगुण (अनुकूल) वासनाओं की ही अभिव्यक्ति होती है।
sutras.practical_application: Only those tendencies manifest that match current karmic conditions. Understand that not all latent patterns are active at once.

sutras.english_translation: Memory and impressions maintain continuity even across separations of birth, place, and time, because they are identical in form.
sutras.hindi_meaning: जाति, देश और काल से व्यवहित (पृथक्) होने पर भी स्मृति और संस्कार के एकरूपत्व से आनन्तर्य (निरन्तरता) बनी रहती है।
sutras.practical_application: Deep patterns persist across vast spans of time and circumstance. Recognize that your strongest tendencies have deep roots and require sustained effort to transform.

sutras.english_translation: Those impressions are beginningless because the desire to live is eternal.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उन (वासनाओं) का अनादित्व है, क्योंकि आशीः (जीवनेच्छा) नित्य है।
sutras.practical_application: The desire to exist is primordial and without beginning. Understanding this deep-rooted nature of desire helps you work with it more skillfully.

sutras.english_translation: Since vasanas are held together by cause, effect, support, and object, when these disappear, so do the vasanas.
sutras.hindi_meaning: हेतु, फल, आश्रय और आलम्बन से संगृहीत होने के कारण, इनके अभाव में उन (वासनाओं) का अभाव होता है।
sutras.practical_application: To dissolve habitual patterns, remove their causes, supports, and objects of attachment. When the conditions disappear, the habit dissolves.

sutras.english_translation: Past and future exist in their own nature, the difference being in the path (time) of the properties.
sutras.hindi_meaning: अतीत और अनागत स्वरूप से विद्यमान हैं; धर्मों के अध्व (काल-मार्ग) के भेद से (भिन्नता है)।
sutras.practical_application: Past and future are not nonexistent; they exist in potential form. Understanding this helps you relate wisely to memory and anticipation.

sutras.english_translation: These properties, whether manifest or subtle, are of the nature of the gunas.
sutras.hindi_meaning: वे (धर्म) व्यक्त या सूक्ष्म होते हैं और गुणात्मक (गुण-स्वरूप) हैं।
sutras.practical_application: All phenomena, whether visible or hidden, are expressions of the three gunas. Understanding this simplifies your view of the entire manifest world.

sutras.english_translation: The reality of an object is due to the uniqueness of the transformation of the gunas.
sutras.hindi_meaning: परिणाम के एकत्व (एक ही रूप में बदलना) से वस्तु की तत्त्वता (सत्यता) है।
sutras.practical_application: Each object is real because it represents a unique configuration of the gunas. Respect the reality of the world while understanding its deeper nature.

sutras.english_translation: The same object is perceived differently by different minds; therefore the mind and the object follow separate paths.
sutras.hindi_meaning: वस्तु की समानता में चित्त के भेद से उन दोनों का मार्ग विभक्त (पृथक्) है।
sutras.practical_application: Different people perceive the same reality differently. Your experience is filtered through your mind. Purify the mind for clearer perception.

sutras.sanskrit: न चैकचित्ततन्त्रं वस्तु तदप्रमाणकं तदा किं स्यात्
sutras.transliteration: na caika-citta-tantraṁ vastu tad apramāṇakaṁ tadā kiṁ syāt
sutras.english_translation: An object does not depend on a single mind; if it did, what would happen when that mind did not perceive it?
sutras.hindi_meaning: वस्तु एक चित्त पर निर्भर नहीं है; यदि होती तो जब वह चित्त उसे न जानता तब वह क्या होती?
sutras.practical_application: The world exists independently of your perception of it. This refutes solipsism and grounds you in a shared, objective reality.

sutras.english_translation: An object is known or unknown depending on whether the mind is colored by it.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उस (वस्तु) के उपराग (प्रभाव) की अपेक्षा से चित्त के लिए वस्तु ज्ञात या अज्ञात होती है।
sutras.practical_application: You only know what your mind attends to. Expand your awareness to perceive more of reality, and purify the mind to perceive more clearly.

sutras.english_translation: The modifications of the mind are always known to the lord Purusha, because of its changeless nature.
sutras.hindi_meaning: चित्तवृत्तियाँ सदा उसके प्रभु पुरुष को ज्ञात रहती हैं, क्योंकि पुरुष अपरिणामी (अपरिवर्तनशील) है।
sutras.practical_application: Pure consciousness witnesses all mental activity without itself being affected. Identify with this unchanging witness rather than with passing thoughts.

sutras.english_translation: The mind is not self-luminous, because it is an object of perception.
sutras.hindi_meaning: वह (चित्त) स्वाभास (स्वप्रकाश) नहीं है, क्योंकि वह दृश्य (देखा जाने वाला) है।
sutras.practical_application: The mind is an instrument, not the source of awareness. It is lit by consciousness, just as the moon is lit by the sun.

sutras.english_translation: The mind cannot perceive both itself and another object simultaneously.
sutras.hindi_meaning: एक समय में दोनों (स्वयं और विषय) का अवधारण (ग्रहण) नहीं हो सकता।
sutras.practical_application: The mind can focus on only one thing at a time. This limitation proves it is not pure consciousness. True awareness transcends this limitation.

sutras.english_translation: If one mind were perceived by another, there would be an infinite regress and confusion of memory.
sutras.hindi_meaning: यदि एक चित्त दूसरे चित्त से दृश्य हो तो बुद्धि की बुद्धि का अतिप्रसंग (अनन्त श्रृंखला) और स्मृति-संकर (स्मृतियों का मिश्रण) हो जायगा।
sutras.practical_application: There must be one unchanging awareness behind all mental activity. Logical analysis confirms the existence of a witnessing consciousness beyond the mind.

sutras.english_translation: When the unchanging consciousness assumes the form of the intellect, self-knowledge becomes possible.
sutras.hindi_meaning: चिति (चेतन-शक्ति) की अप्रतिसंक्रमा (अपरिवर्तनशीलता) से तदाकारापत्ति (उसके आकार को धारण करना) होने पर स्वबुद्धि-संवेदन (अपनी बुद्धि का ज्ञान) होता है।
sutras.practical_application: Pure consciousness does not change but appears to take on the form of whatever it illumines. Recognize this principle to understand how self-awareness works.

sutras.english_translation: The mind, colored by both the Seer and the seen, comprehends everything.
sutras.hindi_meaning: द्रष्टा और दृश्य दोनों से उपरक्त (रंगा हुआ) चित्त सर्वार्थ (सब कुछ समझने वाला) होता है।
sutras.practical_application: The mind serves as a bridge between consciousness and the world. Its unique position allows it to reflect both, making complete understanding possible.

sutras.transliteration: tad asaṁkhyeya-vāsanābhiś citram api parārthaṁ saṁhatya-kāritvāt
sutras.english_translation: Though variegated by countless vasanas, the mind acts for the sake of another (Purusha), because it operates in conjunction.
sutras.hindi_meaning: वह (चित्त) असंख्य वासनाओं से चित्रित (विविध) होने पर भी परार्थ (पुरुष के लिए) है, क्योंकि संहत्यकारी (मिलकर कार्य करने वाला) है।
sutras.practical_application: Despite its complexity, the mind exists to serve your liberation. Use it as a tool for awakening rather than being enslaved by its patterns.

sutras.english_translation: For one who sees the distinction, the contemplation of the nature of the self ceases.
sutras.hindi_meaning: विशेष (आत्मा और चित्त का भेद) देखने वाले की आत्मभावभावना (अहंभावना) की निवृत्ति हो जाती है।
sutras.practical_application: When you truly see the difference between the Self and the mind, the seeking itself drops away. There is nothing more to figure out.

sutras.english_translation: Then the mind inclines toward discrimination and gravitates toward liberation.
sutras.hindi_meaning: तब विवेक की ओर झुका हुआ, कैवल्य की ओर गतिमान चित्त होता है।
sutras.practical_application: At an advanced stage, the mind naturally flows toward discrimination and freedom. Trust this momentum and do not create obstacles.

sutras.english_translation: In the intervals of that discrimination, other thoughts arise from residual impressions.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उसके छिद्रों (अन्तरालों) में संस्कारों से अन्य प्रत्यय (विचार) उत्पन्न होते हैं।
sutras.practical_application: Even near liberation, old patterns can resurface in moments of inattention. Remain vigilant until all impressions are fully dissolved.

sutras.english_translation: The removal of these is like the removal of the kleshas, as has been described.
sutras.hindi_meaning: इनका हान (नाश) क्लेशों के समान (पूर्वोक्त विधि से) कहा गया है।
sutras.practical_application: Apply the same methods used to remove afflictions: discrimination, meditation, and detachment. The approach is consistent throughout the path.

sutras.transliteration: prasaṁkhyāne'py akusīdasya sarvathā viveka-khyāter dharma-meghaḥ samādhiḥ
sutras.english_translation: For one who has no interest even in the highest knowledge and maintains constant discrimination, the Dharma Megha (Cloud of Virtue) Samadhi arises.
sutras.hindi_meaning: प्रसंख्यान (सर्वोच्च ज्ञान) में भी अकुसीद (निस्पृह) रहने वाले को सर्वथा विवेकख्याति से धर्ममेघ समाधि प्राप्त होती है।
sutras.practical_application: When even the desire for the highest knowledge is surrendered, the Cloud of Virtue rains down supreme grace. Let go of all seeking, even spiritual seeking.

sutras.english_translation: From that, the cessation of afflictions and karma follows.
sutras.hindi_meaning: उससे क्लेश और कर्म की निवृत्ति होती है।
sutras.practical_application: In the state of dharma megha samadhi, all afflictions and karmic consequences come to an end. This is the beginning of absolute freedom.

sutras.english_translation: Then, with all coverings and impurities removed, the infinity of knowledge makes what remains to be known seem small.
sutras.hindi_meaning: तब सर्व आवरण और मल से रहित ज्ञान की अनन्तता से ज्ञेय (जानने योग्य) अल्प (कम) रह जाता है।
sutras.practical_application: When all mental impurities are removed, knowledge becomes infinite. What seemed like a vast unknown becomes trivially small in the light of pure awareness.

sutras.english_translation: Then the gunas, having fulfilled their purpose, end their sequence of transformation.
sutras.hindi_meaning: तब कृतार्थ (कार्य पूरा कर चुके) गुणों के परिणाम-क्रम की समाप्ति हो जाती है।
sutras.practical_application: When the gunas have served their purpose of liberating the soul, the cycle of change ceases for that individual. Nature's work is done.

sutras.english_translation: Sequence is the counterpart of moments, perceivable at the end of a series of transformations.
sutras.hindi_meaning: क्षण-प्रतियोगी (क्षण के विपरीत), परिणाम के अपरान्त (अन्त) में निर्ग्राह्य (ग्रहण करने योग्य) क्रम है।
sutras.practical_application: Time and sequence are understood only at their conclusion. From the vantage point of liberation, the entire series of transformations is seen as one.

sutras.sanskrit: पुरुषार्थशून्यानां गुणानां प्रतिप्रसवः कैवल्यं स्वरूपप्रतिष्ठा वा चितिशक्तेरिति
sutras.transliteration: puruṣārtha-śūnyānāṁ guṇānāṁ pratiprasavaḥ kaivalyaṁ svarūpa-pratiṣṭhā vā citi-śakter iti
sutras.english_translation: Kaivalya is the involution of the gunas, no longer serving the purpose of the Purusha, or it is the establishment of the power of consciousness in its own nature.
sutras.hindi_meaning: पुरुषार्थ-शून्य गुणों का प्रतिप्रसव (अपने कारण में लीन होना) ही कैवल्य है, अथवा चिति-शक्ति (चेतन-शक्ति) की स्वरूपप्रतिष्ठा (अपने स्वरूप में स्थिति) कैवल्य है।
sutras.practical_application: Liberation is the gunas returning to their source and consciousness resting in its own nature. This is the final goal: you are free, whole, and complete as pure awareness.
